raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing {$3000.00} in unauthorized charges on my JetBlue Mastercard Account. 
The bank denied my fraud claim based on the false statement that " the card was in my possession. '' -The denial reason ( " card in possession '' ) is factually impossible as the card used for fraud was never delivered to me. 
Facts : I have been a customer since XX/XX/XXXX with a good payment history. -The bank mailed a replacement card to my address via regular, non-secure postal mail, which I never received. The fraudulent transactions were made on the new card number. 
I reported the fraud as soon as I became aware of it, about XX/XX/XXXX, stating which specific charges were fraud. 
I stated that the OLD card was not physically stolen. I clearly stated that I never had the new card The fraudulent charges were on the NEW card. 

As per the Fair Credit Billing Act I am not liable for fraudulent charges. The burden of proof lies with the card issuer to demonstrate that the transaction was authorized by the cardholder. 
They have failed to prove I received the card used for these transactions. 
Sending a replacement card via regular mail without secure delivery protocols creates a foreseeable risk of theft in transit, for which the issuer bears responsibility. 
Current Status : The bank has denied my claim and refuses to reverse the charges despite my clear explanation. -Their agent was unhelpful and provided inaccurate information. 
If the complainee continues to make false claims I will pursue this issue with the Attorney General 's office and through legal means.
